title: VISITING THE PHARMACY
conversation: Ann goes to the pharmacy to get medicine for her husband, who has a rash on his back. She talks to Ken, the pharmacist. Ann: My husband has a rash on his back. It's driving him nuts. Ken: When did the rash break out? Ann: Yesterday morning. What do you think it could be? Ken: It could be any number of things. Ann: Such as? Ken: For starters, it could be an allergic reaction to something. Ann: I recently started using a new brand of laundry detergent. You may have hit the nail on the head. Ken: If it is small red dots, it may be hives. Ann: What do you recommend he take for it? Ken: Is he on anything now? Ann: No. Ken: Try an over-the-counter anti-itch cream or a pill like Claritin. Ann: What if that doesn't work? What if it gets worse? Ken: If it doesn't clear up, he should see a doctor. It's probably nothing serious, but better safe than sorry. Ann: Right! We should nip this in the bud.

idioms.meaning_en: to make someone very annoyed or crazy
idioms.meaning_vi: làm ai đó phát điên hoặc cực kỳ khó chịu
idioms.example: The constant sound of the car alarm was driving him nuts.

idioms.meaning_en: to suddenly develop a skin condition like a rash or pimples
idioms.example: I tend to break out whenever I eat too much chocolate.
idioms.context: When did the rash break out?

idioms.meaning_en: a large but unspecified quantity; many
idioms.meaning_vi: rất nhiều; bất kỳ số lượng nào
idioms.example: There are any number of reasons why the project might fail.
idioms.context: It could be any number of things.

idioms.meaning_en: as a first point; to begin with
idioms.example: For starters, we need to find a place to stay before we book the flights.
idioms.context: For starters, it could be an allergic reaction to something.

idioms.meaning_en: a physical response to a substance to which the body is sensitive
idioms.example: He had a severe allergic reaction to the bee sting.
idioms.context: For starters, it could be an allergic reaction to something.

idioms.idiom: hit the nail on the head
idioms.meaning_en: to describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
idioms.meaning_vi: nói đúng trọng tâm; đoán trúng phóc
idioms.example: She really hit the nail on the head when she said the team lacks leadership.
idioms.context: You may have hit the nail on the head.

idioms.example: The doctor put her on antibiotics for her ear infection.

idioms.meaning_en: medicine sold directly to a consumer without a prescription
idioms.example: You can buy most pain relievers over-the-counter at any pharmacy.
idioms.context: Try an over-the-counter anti-itch cream or a pill like Claritin.

idioms.meaning_en: to disappear or go away (referring to a medical condition)
idioms.meaning_vi: khỏi hẳn; hết sạch (vết ban, mụn, bệnh)
idioms.example: The doctor said the infection should clear up in about a week.
idioms.context: If it doesn't clear up, he should see a doctor.

idioms.meaning_en: it is better to be careful than to take a risk and regret it later
idioms.meaning_vi: cẩn tắc vô áy náy; thà cẩn thận còn hơn hối hận
idioms.example: I think I'll bring an extra jacket, better safe than sorry.
idioms.context: It's probably nothing serious, but better safe than sorry.

idioms.meaning_en: to stop a problem at an early stage before it becomes serious
idioms.meaning_vi: ngăn chặn từ trong trứng nước
idioms.example: The company decided to nip the PR problem in the bud by issuing an immediate apology.
idioms.context: Right! We should nip this in the bud.
